summary: Follow-up cleanup for recent vi decompilation work. The PR made vi0601 matching and linked, replaced several speculative/static data artifacts with literals or correct symbol scopes, removed multiple vi*.static.h files, corrected archive/symbol names, and used proper HSD JObj accessors/setters to improve matching across vi0401, vi0501, vi0502, vi0601, vi0801, vi1101, vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202. decomp.dev reported +968 matched-code bytes, +1700 linked-code bytes, and 3 new matches, while noting one broken match in vi1201v1::un_803204B0 due to moving that function to vi1201v2.
what_changed: Marked src/melee/vi/vi0601.c as Matching in configure.py, replacing Object(NonMatching, "melee/vi/vi0601.c") with Object(Matching, "melee/vi/vi0601.c").

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to correct symbol scopes and data splits, including making vi0601_CameraCallback local and converting several generated un_/it_ data symbols into local @NNN constants for strings, floats, and doubles.

Deleted several per-file static headers: vi0501.static.h, vi1101.static.h, vi1201v1.static.h, vi1201v2.static.h, and vi1202.static.h. Their useful extern/static declarations were moved into the corresponding C files or public headers where needed.

Replaced hallucinated or over-abstracted string data with direct archive and scene symbol literals, such as "Vi0501.dat"/"visual0501Scene", "Vi0502.dat"/"visual0502Scene", "IrAls.dat", "Vi0601.dat"/"visual0601Scene", "Vi0801.dat"/"visual0801Scene", "Vi1201v1.dat"/"visual1201v1Scene", "TyKoopa.dat"/"ToyKoopaModel_TopN_joint", and "GmRgStnd.dat"/"standScene".

Resolved many floating-point static variables into literal constants where the diff shows direct values: 0.0f, 0.5f, 0.55f, 0.65f, 1.0f, -1.0f, 3.0f, 60.0f, 70.0f, 75.0f, 85.0f, 98.0f, 100.0f, 108.0f, 120.0f, 123.0f, 130.0f, 160.0f, 170.0f, 190.0f, 241.0f, 251.0f, and 271.0f.
Changed vi0401 to treat un_804D6F58 as an HSD_JObj* and retrieve the player spawn position through HSD_JObjGetTranslation2 instead of defining a custom struct with a Vec3 at offset 0x38.
Adjusted vi0601 to use HSD_JObjGetScaleX(child) and literal animation frame 0.0f instead of static float symbols, and to load "Vi0601.dat" directly.

Reworked vi1201v1 scene setup by replacing a large speculative Vi1201SceneData data struct with direct literals, a player_spawn Vec3, JObj accessor calls, and helper wrappers for JObj translate/scale/rotation writes that call ftCo_800C6AFC under matching conditions.
Moved un_803204B0 out of vi1201v1.c and into vi1201v2.c, adding its declaration to vi1201v2.h and removing it from vi1201v1.h.
Corrected src/melee/gm/gm_1BA8.c to include <melee/vi/vi1201v2.h> instead of <melee/vi/vi1201v1.h>, consistent with un_803204B0 moving to vi1201v2.
Changed pl_8003FDC8 from void pl_8003FDC8(int arg0) to void pl_8003FDC8(int arg0, int arg1) in both plbonuslib.c and plbonuslib.h; vi1202 now calls it with fighter kind/entity data and port.
Updated vi1202 function signatures: un_80321EBC now takes u32 for its first argument, and un_8032201C now returns bool instead of void.
smart_moves: The PR replaced guessed global string arrays and packed data structs with direct lbArchive_LoadSymbols literals, which both fixed data layout/symbol-map issues and made the intended scene/archive names searchable.
Using real HSD JObj APIs such as HSD_JObjGetTranslation2, HSD_JObjGetScaleX, and HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z avoided hand-rolled struct offsets and raw field mutations that were hurting matches.
Removing static float declarations and using literal frame/scale values reduced fake sdata2 dependencies and improved data matching in multiple vi translation units.
Moving un_803204B0 to vi1201v2, then updating gm_1BA8.c and vi1201v2.h, kept function ownership aligned with its actual code unit rather than preserving a stale declaration in vi1201v1.
The build config change to mark vi0601 Matching was supported by code and data cleanups in the same PR, not just by configure.py.
The custom vi1201v1 JObj setter wrappers preserve assert/dirty-matrix side effects needed for codegen, showing a practical tactic when normal library helpers do not produce the expected match.
decomp_lessons: Do not preserve hallucinated static data just because it fills a section. When archive names, scene symbols, frame constants, or scale constants are known, direct literals can improve both code and data matching.
Prefer HSD accessor/helper calls over manually reading JObj fields when the original likely called the helper. This matters for both generated code shape and side effects such as matrix dirtying.

Per-file .static.h headers are a temporary convenience, not a goal. As functions mature, move declarations into the C file or real public headers and delete static headers to avoid exposing fake locals.
Symbol-map scope matters. Local constants should be represented with local @NNN symbols rather than forced global un_/it_ names when the compiler emitted local data.
Correct function ownership can temporarily look like a regression in decomp.dev if a match moves between units; record the move and update includes/headers to make the ownership clear.

Function signatures should reflect actual call ABI even when parameters are unused in the current function body; pl_8003FDC8 gaining a second int and vi1202 functions changing return/argument types are examples.
naming_conventions: Local compiler-generated constants in symbols.txt use @NNN names with scope:local, e.g. @149, @180, @203, @233, @394.

Archive/scene string literals use descriptive asset names such as "Vi0501.dat", "visual0501Scene", "Vi0601.dat", "visual0601Scene", "Vi1201v1.dat", and "visual1201v1Scene" rather than un_8040xxxx placeholder arrays.
Existing unmatched function naming remains address-based where no semantic name is known, e.g. un_8031FD18_OnEnter, fn_8031FAA8, un_8032201C.
The PR retains vi-prefixed file/function organization for visual intro/wait modules, including vi0501, vi0502, vi0601, vi0801, vi1101, vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202.
Scene-related external globals still use address-based un_804Dxxxx names when no better name is established, but their declarations are kept closer to their implementation files.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace static f32/f64 symbols with immediate float literals to remove artificial sdata2 references and match compiler constant handling.
Use HSD_JObjGetTranslation2 instead of struct-offset Vec3 reads for JObj translation.
Use HSD_JObjGetScaleX before HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z to match scale update code in vi0601.
Use HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z calls instead of manual jobj->scale writes when the original appears to call baselib setters.
Introduce local helper wrappers for JObj setters when the desired codegen requires direct field writes plus ftCo_800C6AFC rather than the standard baselib dirty-matrix path.
Prefer goto-based skip/early-return restructuring in vi1202::un_80321EBC where it produces closer control flow than multiple direct returns.
Use PAD_STACK for stack padding in vi1201v1 instead of raw char pad arrays when matching stack layout.
Correct function return types and argument signedness/width, such as bool return for un_8032201C and u32 first parameter for un_80321EBC, to align callsites and generated comparisons.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 49.23% (+0.02%, +968 bytes), linked code 29.82% (+0.04%, +1700 bytes), and linked data 28.63% (+0.01%, +72 bytes).
decomp.dev listed 3 new matches: vi0601::fn_8031E800, vi0401::un_8031D030, and vi1201v1::fn_8031FAA8.
decomp.dev listed one broken match: vi1201v1::un_803204B0 changed from 100.00% to 0.00%, but the diff shows the function was removed from vi1201v1 and added to vi1201v2.

decomp.dev listed 15 improvements in unmatched functions/data, including vi1201v1::un_8031FD18_OnEnter, vi1201v1 .data, vi1202::un_8032201C, vi1202::un_80321EBC, vi0501 .sdata2/.data, vi0502 .data, vi0601 .data/.sdata/.sdata2, and vi0401 .sdata.
decomp.dev also reported three regressions in unmatched data areas: vi0401 .data, vi1201v1 .sdata2, and vi0501 .sdata.
follow_up_queries: Find other vi files that still include *.static.h or declare fake static f32 constants for frame numbers.
Search for remaining lbArchive_LoadSymbols calls that pass un_8040xxxx string arrays instead of known archive/scene literals.
Audit symbols.txt for global un_/it_ string or float constants that should become local @NNN symbols.
Check whether vi1201v1::un_803204B0 was intentionally moved to vi1201v2 and whether decomp.dev should treat the old-unit broken match as acceptable.
Look for other manual JObj field reads/writes that can be replaced by HSD_JObjGetTranslation2, HSD_JObjGetScaleX, or setter helpers.
Review callsites of pl_8003FDC8 to ensure the added second parameter is consistently meaningful and not just ABI padding.
